---
title: "Impact & Ground Work"
description: "Conservation outcomes, community engagement, and field work from Tanzania's coastline"
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-hero">
  <p class="hero-tagline">From the reef to the community — real outcomes on the Swahili Coast</p>
</div>
```

## Work on the Ground

My conservation work spans Tanzania's coastline — from the coral reefs of Pemba and Zanzibar to the mangrove estuaries of the mainland. Every data point I collect represents a reef, a species, or a community that depends on a healthy ocean.

---

## Key Impact Areas

::: {.impact-grid}

::: {.impact-area-card}
### 🐠 Biodiversity Monitoring
Systematic ecological surveys tracking reef fish, invertebrates, corals, seagrass, and mangroves across multiple sites. Data feeds directly into conservation plans used by local government and NGO partners.

**Outcomes:**
- Long-term baseline datasets established at 7+ coastal sites
- Species richness trends documented across 3 ecosystem types
- Monitoring protocols adopted by community rangers
:::

::: {.impact-area-card}
### 🌿 Habitat Conservation
Supporting designation and management of locally managed marine areas (LMMAs) by equipping communities with data on habitat extent, health, and ecosystem services.

**Outcomes:**
- Contributed data to support marine protected area (MPA) boundary proposals
- Seagrass and mangrove extent mapped using GIS tools
- Coral bleaching events documented and reported for early response
:::

::: {.impact-area-card}
### 👥 Community Engagement
Working directly with fishing communities to co-design monitoring programs, build data literacy, and connect local ecological knowledge with scientific methods.

**Outcomes:**
- Trained 12+ community data collectors across coastal villages
- Co-facilitated multi-stakeholder conservation planning workshops
- Built trust and long-term partnerships with fishing community leaders
:::

::: {.impact-area-card}
### 📈 Data for Decision-Making
Transforming raw field data into reports, dashboards, and visualizations that influence conservation policy and attract research funding.

**Outcomes:**
- Annual biodiversity reports produced for government and NGO stakeholders
- Data pipelines built in R for automated reporting
- Contributed datasets to regional biodiversity databases
:::

:::

---

## Conservation Timeline

```{=html}
<div class="timeline">

  <div class="timeline-item left">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2019</span>
      <h3>Joined Mwambao Coastal Community Network</h3>
      <p>Began ecological monitoring fieldwork along Tanzania's northern coast, establishing baseline biodiversity data for key coastal sites.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-item right">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2020</span>
      <h3>Community Training Programme Launched</h3>
      <p>Led the design and rollout of a community-based monitoring programme, training local fishers as citizen scientists to collect reef health data.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-item left">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2021</span>
      <h3>First Documentary Participation</h3>
      <p>Featured in a documentary production covering marine conservation efforts on the Swahili Coast — bringing scientific field work to a wider audience.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-item right">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2022</span>
      <h3>Regional Data Infrastructure Built</h3>
      <p>Developed R-based data pipelines and dashboards integrating monitoring data from multiple sites for annual reporting to government and NGO partners.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-item left">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2023</span>
      <h3>MPA Support & GIS Mapping</h3>
      <p>Provided spatial analysis and ecological data to support marine protected area planning — including seagrass and mangrove extent mapping using QGIS and R.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-item right">
    <div class="timeline-content">
      <span class="timeline-year">2024 – Present</span>
      <h3>Ecological Data Manager</h3>
      <p>Leading data management strategy at Mwambao, overseeing biodiversity monitoring across 7+ sites, managing databases, and mentoring field teams.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```

---

## Field Work Gallery

```{=html}
<p class="gallery-note">📸 Photos from the field — surveys, community workshops, and coastal monitoring sites</p>

<div class="gallery-placeholder">
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">🤿</div>
    <p>Reef Survey, Pemba</p>
  </div>
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">🌿</div>
    <p>Mangrove Transect</p>
  </div>
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">👥</div>
    <p>Community Workshop</p>
  </div>
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">📊</div>
    <p>Data Collection Training</p>
  </div>
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">🐠</div>
    <p>Fish Count Survey</p>
  </div>
  <div class="gallery-slot">
    <div class="gallery-icon">🗺️</div>
    <p>GIS Mapping Session</p>
  </div>
</div>

<p class="gallery-note" style="margin-top:1rem;">
  <em>Gallery images coming soon — reach out via <a href="mailto:lauriankaijage@gmail.com">email</a> to request field photos.</em>
</p>
```

---

## Partners & Collaborators

::: {.partners-row}
- **Mwambao Coastal Community Network** — primary employer and implementing partner
- **Local Government Authorities** — data sharing and marine spatial planning
- **Regional Research Institutions** — joint biodiversity assessments
- **Fishing Communities** — co-management and citizen science
- **International NGO Partners** — funding, technical support, and documentary production
:::
